generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatBed : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "A 214×214×3 mm surface heat source providing up to 100W of power to heat the air inside the styrofoam box"
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "A 12V/24V fan circulating air at 0.02 m³/s to ensure uniform temperature distribution from bottom to top"
]

instance MosfetController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "MOSFETs and optocouplers on PCB to isolate Raspberry Pi GPIO signals from high-current heatbed actuation (4A)"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance RoomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring room temperature (external to the styrofoam box)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RoomTemperatureDataStream
]

instance BoxTopTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor placed at hottest position inside the styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BoxTopTemperatureDataStream
]

instance BoxBottomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor placed at coldest position inside the styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BoxBottomTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance RoomTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RoomTemperatureSensor
]

instance BoxTopTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BoxTopTemperatureSensor
]

instance BoxBottomTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BoxBottomTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Single-parameter model predicting temperature change via dT_bair/dt = [V×I×Δt − G_box×(T_bair − T_room)] / C_air"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Four-parameter model accounting for heatbed thermal inertia: C_air, C_heater, G_box, G_heater"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings (room/air) from DS18S20 sensors with timestamps in Kelvin/Celsius"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RoomTemperatureDataStream, BoxTopTemperatureDataStream, BoxBottomTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonSciPyEnabler
]

instance PowerInputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Power input (V×I×Δt) from the heatbed"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RabbitMQEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelA, ModelB
]

instance ExperimentalTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Experimental temperature trajectories used for calibration validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RabbitMQEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonSciPyEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker for real-time synchronization between physical and digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamEnabler, ControlCommandEnabler
]

instance LowLevelDriverEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-level driver for sensor/actuator abstraction and safety protocols"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamEnabler, ControlCommandEnabler
]

instance PythonSciPyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python (SciPy) for non-linear least squares calibration of model parameters"
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]

instance StatechartEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Statechart implementation for bang-bang control logic (e.g., Fig. 8)"
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyMonitoringService
]

instance DataStreamEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for real-time temperature data streaming from sensors"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperaturePredictionService, UniformityValidationService
]

instance ControlCommandEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for event-driven actuation feedback from controller commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance TemperaturePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Two dynamic models (Model A: single-parameter Model B: four-parameter) predict temperature inside the box using energy flow equations"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAPredictionInsight, ModelBPredictionInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Non-linear least squares optimization calibrates model parameters (C_air, G_box, C_heater, G_heater)"
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibrationResultsAction
]

instance UniformityValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization of temperature uniformity via sensor data (max discrepancy of 7°C/6°C with fan voltages)"
    DTDFVocab:provides UniformityInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Algorithms detect deviations from target temperature (LL/UL thresholds) and trigger corrective actions"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyAlertAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ModelAPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Single-parameter model forecast of air temperature inside the box for proactive adjustments"
]

instance ModelBPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Four-parameter model forecast accounting for heatbed thermal inertia and improved accuracy"
]

instance UniformityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Temperature distribution validation showing max discrepancy of 6°C with optimized fan voltage"
]

instance CalibrationResultsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Parameter updates (C_air, G_box) improving prediction accuracy via non-linear least squares optimization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggered when temperature deviates from LL/UL thresholds, initiating fan/heatbed modulation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: System Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HeatBed, Fan, MosfetController,
                 RoomTemperatureSensor, BoxTopTemperatureSensor, BoxBottomTemperatureSensor,
                 RaspberryPiController
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B handling GPIO, Wi-Fi/Bluetooth, and low-level driver coordination"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** is implemented via:
  - **Control commands**: The DT sends actuation commands (ON/OFF) to the **heatbed** and **fan** through the **RabbitMQ server**, which the low-level driver interprets and executes.
  - **State synchronization**: The DT receives updated physical states (e.g., sensor readings, actuator positions) via RabbitMQ, ensuring real-time alignment between the PT and DT.
  - **Control strategy**: The Raspberry Pi’s **bang-bang controller** (with configurable limits: LL, UL, H, C) processes virtual commands to regulate temperature dynamically."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale involves:
  - **Data acquisition**: Periodic sampling (unspecified frequency) of temperature data via the low-level driver.
  - **Model calibration**: Offline optimization (experiment → calibration → validation) with no explicit real-time synchronization. The DT processes data in discrete steps (e.g., calibration iterations), with no mention of dynamic updates during operation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **DT constellation** is structured as:
  - **Centralized architecture**: All components (sensors, actuators, controller) reside on the **Raspberry Pi**, with RabbitMQ acting as a middleware layer for inter-component communication.
  - **Data flow**:
    - Physical sensors → RabbitMQ → DT (for monitoring/calibration).
    - DT → RabbitMQ → Low-level driver → Physical actuators (heatbed/fan).
  - **Validation loop**: The DT continuously compares simulated predictions (Model A/B) with real-time sensor data to update parameters dynamically."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The **life-cycle stages** for the Digital Twin are:
  - **Design phase**: Models (Model A/B) are developed based on assumptions (e.g., uniform temperature distribution, constant specific heat capacity).
  - **Manufacturing phase**: Hardware (styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, sensors) is assembled and validated via uniformity experiments (e.g., sensor placement, fan voltage tests).
  - **Operational phase**: The DT runs in real-time, providing predictions, calibration, and safety monitoring during incubator operation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **fidelity and validity considerations** include:
  - **Model assumptions**:
    - **Assumption 1**: Temperature uniformity is validated via experiments (max discrepancy: 7°C/6°C with fan voltages of 12V/18V).
    - **Assumption 2**: Box walls are assumed non-heating (verified via experiments).
    - **Assumption 3**: Constant specific heat capacity of air (700 J/kg·K) is justified.
    - **Assumption 4**: Heatbed/fan heat losses are ignored (Model B relaxes this).
  - **Validation mechanisms**:
    - **Experimental comparison**: Models are validated against real-time sensor data (e.g., Fig. 10).
    - **Parameter calibration**: Non-linear least squares ensures minimal residual error (~0% in experiments)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between PT and DT uses:
  - **RabbitMQ protocol**: Asynchronous message broker for real-time data exchange (no JSON payloads explicitly mentioned; messages are generic sensor/actuator commands)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted on-premise on the Raspberry Pi 4:
  - **Software stack**: RabbitMQ server (message broker), low-level driver (Python/C++), and calibration tools (SciPy). No mention of external deployment or cloud hosting.
  - **Calibration**: Conducted offline after experiments (not deployed externally)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security/safety considerations include:
  - **Hardware isolation**: MOSFETs/optocouplers prevent Raspberry Pi GPIO from high-current actuation (4A), ensuring safe operation.
  - **Control logic**: Bang-bang controller enforces temperature bounds (LL, UL) and waiting durations (C) to prevent overshoot.
  - **Wireless capability**: Raspberry Pi’s built-in Wi-Fi/Bluetooth enables remote monitoring but no explicit human oversight requirement is stated."
]
